text: Label smoothing is a regularization technique that converts hard one-hot targets into soft probability distributions.
Here's a one-hot vector where the true class has probability one, and all others are zero.
The formula works by mixing the one-hot vector with a uniform distribution using a smoothing parameter epsilon, typically 0.1.
We scale the one-hot target by 0.9 and add 0.02 to all classes from the uniform distribution.
The result is a smoothed distribution where the true class still has the highest probability at 0.92, but other classes get small non-zero values.
This reduces model overconfidence, improves generalization, and prevents the network from becoming too certain about its predictions.
